title: ATAS Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)
meta_title: ATAS Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026) | TradingToolsHub
meta_description: ATAS Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). ATAS (4.3★, Free). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tips. Updated 2026.
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using ATAS — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
What is ATAS?

ATAS is a professional-grade order flow and volume analysis platform designed specifically for traders who want to move beyond traditional candlestick charting and into the deeper mechanics of market structure. Launched in 2015 and currently rated 4.3/5 by active traders, ATAS specializes in footprint chart analysis with 400+ cluster configuration variations, 70+ dedicated volume tools, and direct connections to 25+ global exchanges including CME, NYSE, NASDAQ, and major cryptocurrency platforms. The platform serves futures scalpers, order flow traders, and crypto analysts who need institutional-grade Smart DOM (depth of market) and Smart Tape (filtered tape reading) tools. Whether you're analyzing Bitcoin microstructure for free on ATAS's crypto tier ($0/month) or scaling ES contracts with the Pro plan ($69.95/month), ATAS provides the specialized tooling that volume traders sacrifice standard technical analysis to access.

How to Create Your ATAS Account

Account creation with ATAS is straightforward and designed to get you trading within minutes. Here's the process:

  • Visit the ATAS website: Go to atas.net and locate the "Sign Up" or "Start Free" button, typically positioned in the top navigation bar.
  • Choose your plan: Select from the free tier (limited to crypto markets with delayed replay), Plus ($24.95/month for stocks and futures with limited features), Pro ($69.95/month), or Ultra ($89.95/month for institutional clients). You can start free and upgrade anytime.
  • Enter your email and create a password: ATAS uses a standard email/password verification model. They'll send a confirmation link to your registered email address—check both your inbox and spam folder as verification emails sometimes get caught in filters.
  • Verify your email address: Click the link in the confirmation email to activate your account. This step is mandatory before accessing the platform.
  • Install the ATAS desktop application: ATAS is Windows-only (7, 8, 10, 11) and downloaded as a standalone executable. The installer is approximately 200MB. Mac and Linux users cannot use ATAS natively, though some run it through Windows virtual machines or remote desktops.
  • Login and configure your connection: On first launch, ATAS will ask you to select your data provider (free demo data, paper trading, or live connections to your broker). This choice determines what market data streams into your charts.

Total setup time: 5-10 minutes from email confirmation to launching the application. No phone verification, identity confirmation, or funding account required for free or demo access.

Setting Up ATAS for the First Time

When you first open ATAS, the interface presents a workspace designed for density rather than simplicity. Don't be intimidated—here's what you need to configure immediately:

1. Select Your Data Source and Market

ATAS's main settings screen asks you to choose your connection: free demo data, a broker connection (Rithmic, CQG, Interactive Brokers, etc. for futures/stocks), or Crypto Exchanges API (Binance, OKX, Bybit, Deribit). For crypto traders, the free tier connects directly to exchange order book data. For futures scalpers, you'll connect to a broker-provided data feed (some brokers like Rithmic offer free data with funded accounts). The connection you select here determines every symbol available to you and the quality of tick-by-tick data flowing into your footprint charts.

2. Create Your First Chart with Footprint View

Once connected, right-click on the chart window and select "Chart Type," then choose "Footprint" rather than the default Bars or Candles view. This is ATAS's core offering—a visual representation of every buy and sell order at each price level, displayed as colored clusters. The default configuration (Volume by Price) is suitable for beginners; more advanced traders customize cluster behavior through the 400+ variation options available in the Properties panel.

3. Configure the Smart DOM

The Smart DOM is ATAS's answer to traditional depth-of-market windows. Open it via View > Smart DOM. This real-time window shows cumulative bid/ask liquidity with color intensity representing order concentration. Enable "One-Click Trading" in Smart DOM settings so you can execute trades directly from the DOM window by clicking bid/ask levels—critical for scalpers who need sub-second order entry.

4. Set Up Smart Tape

Smart Tape filters the incoming order tape to highlight large institutional trades, block trades, and unusual order sizes while filtering out noise from small retail orders. Navigate to View > Smart Tape and adjust the "minimum order size" filter based on your market. For ES (S&P 500 E-mini futures), a 50+ contract minimum highlights significant order flow; for crypto, adjust to your exchange's block trade thresholds.

5. Connect Alerts

ATAS alerts trigger when specific volume or price conditions occur. Set up a basic volume alert: right-click a price level on your footprint chart, select "Create Alert," and specify the condition (e.g., "Alert when bid volume exceeds 10,000 contracts"). Alerts can sound an audio alert, pop a window notification, or execute pre-programmed trades if you're running backtests or automated strategies. For beginners, simply enable "Sound Alert" and adjust volume to hear notifications.

6. Customize Your Workspace Layout

ATAS allows multiple charts, DOM windows, and tape feeds on a single screen. Save your first workspace layout: File > Save Workspace As. Name it "Crypto Scalp Setup" or "Futures ES Setup"—you can switch between saved layouts instantly, useful if you trade multiple instruments or strategies simultaneously.

Essential Features You Should Know

1. Footprint Charts and Cluster Analysis

The foundation of ATAS. Each candle's body displays buy (typically green) and sell (typically red) volume as block clusters. Gaps in clusters indicate price levels with no traded volume (areas of rejection). The density of clusters reveals concentration points where large institutional orders accumulate. With 400+ cluster variation options, you can visualize volume by price, delta (buy volume minus sell volume), cumulative delta, or custom calculations. Beginners use the standard configuration; advanced traders build custom cluster profiles to spot hidden support/resistance.

2. Smart DOM (One-Click Trading)

A real-time depth window that shows bid/ask liquidity in color-coded intensity. Lighter colors indicate sparse orders; darker colors indicate heavy clustering. Click directly on any price level in the DOM to place a limit buy order at that price—essential for scalpers who execute dozens of trades per session. The DOM also displays your current position size and average entry price alongside market structure, keeping trade management in a single window.

3. Smart Tape with Filtering

Displays every executed trade (bid or ask) in real time as a scrolling list. Most order flow platforms show tape in green (buy) and red (sell), but ATAS's Smart Tape filters out retail-sized orders and highlights only large institutional block trades. Set your minimum filter to 50 contracts on ES, and you'll see only significant order flow—this is the signal professional traders use to anticipate price moves before they appear on chart.

4. Alerts and Notifications with Automation

Set alerts on volume conditions, price levels, delta accumulation, or custom indicators. ATAS alerts can trigger audio notifications, desktop popups, or automated trade execution in replay mode. For example: "Alert when cumulative delta exceeds 10,000 contracts"—when that condition occurs during a live session or backtest, ATAS notifies you immediately. Advanced users combine alerts with API access to route alerts to external systems (Slack, Discord, or automated trading engines).

5. Backtesting and Replay

ATAS's "Replay" feature lets you step through historical price action minute-by-minute or tick-by-tick with full order book and tape data, as if you're watching a live session from the past. Set up a trading strategy, replay a volatile 2-hour window from last week, and count how many setups your system identified and how many would have profited. The Pro and Ultra plans include data history sufficient for months of replay backtesting.

6. Custom Indicators and API Access

ATAS includes 240+ built-in indicators specifically developed for order flow traders (not generic technical indicators). Build custom indicators using the built-in formula language, or integrate external signals via API. The Plus plan ($24.95/month) includes API access, allowing you to send alerts from ATAS to your own trading bots or receive external signals into ATAS for backtesting.

7. Performance Analytics and Journaling

Log every trade with entry reason, exit reason, and notes in the built-in journal. ATAS calculates your win rate, average win/loss, Sharpe ratio, and drawdown across your journal entries. This data is critical for improving your trading—you can filter journal entries by time of day, market condition, or instrument and see which scenarios consistently profit and which consistently lose.

ATAS Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

Free ($0/month): Real-time crypto order flow data only. Full footprint chart access, Smart DOM, Smart Tape, and replay function limited to crypto markets with 1-minute delayed data during replay. Ideal for cryptocurrency scalpers wanting to test footprint trading without financial commitment. You can actually trade live with this tier—no demo-only restrictions for crypto.

Plus ($24.95/month): Adds stocks and futures markets with 1-month historical replay data. Includes API access for custom integrations, up to 3 simultaneous chart windows, and email support. Best for beginner-to-intermediate traders testing order flow strategies without committing to premium pricing. Limited to one workstation login.

Pro ($69.95/month): Full access to all 70+ volume tools, unlimited historical replay data (6+ months), 6 simultaneous chart windows, priority email and chat support, and advanced alerting. The most popular tier among serious day traders and scalpers. Includes professional-grade charting templates and institutional-level performance analytics in your journal.

Ultra ($89.95/month): Everything in Pro, plus dedicated account support, white-glabel customization options, unlimited workstation logins (trade from multiple computers simultaneously), and direct access to ATAS technical team for custom integrations. Designed for trading firms, hedge funds, and professional traders managing client accounts. The additional $20/month above Pro is primarily for multi-user licensing.

Recommendation by trader type:

  • Crypto scalpers: Start with Free tier. If you're consistently profitable, upgrade to Plus ($24.95) for historical replay to backtest your strategies.
  • Futures scalpers testing order flow: Start with Plus ($24.95) for 1-month replay history and simultaneous 3-chart capability. Once you've validated your setups, upgrade to Pro ($69.95) for unlimited data history and 6-window workspace.
  • Professional traders or trading firms: Pro ($69.95) is the floor—you need unlimited replay data and advanced journaling for accountability. Consider Ultra ($89.95) only if you trade from multiple workstations or require white-label integration.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of ATAS

  • Use footprint configuration presets as market condition filters. Create three saved footprint configurations: one for low-liquidity consolidation (cluster size = 5), one for trending moves (cluster size = 50), and one for breakout volatility (cluster size = 100). Switch between them as market microstructure changes. This single adjustment dramatically improves pattern recognition across different market conditions without reloading the platform.
  • Combine Smart Tape minimum filter size with time-of-day alerts. Institutional order flow differs by session: U.S. equity market opens have different block trade patterns than 2 p.m. consolidation or cash settlement hours. Create different Smart Tape filters for each session and save them as named templates. Load the appropriate template at your market's open to immediately see session-specific order flow patterns.
  • Build a replay-testing workflow before trading live. Spend 2-3 weeks in Replay mode with 10 setups per day on the timeframe and market you want to trade live. Log every setup in your journal before taking a single live trade. This generates historical win/loss data that informs your position sizing and helps you avoid overtrading low-probability setups once live trading begins.
  • Use API alerts to monitor markets while away from your desk. Integrate ATAS alerts with Slack or Telegram: set an alert for "daily delta exceeding 50,000 on ES" and route it to your phone. You don't need to watch the DOM all day, but you'll know immediately when significant order flow imbalances occur—often 20-30 minutes before the candle closes.
  • Master the footprint cluster properties panel for your specific market. The 400+ cluster variations are overwhelming until you pick one market (ES, Bitcoin, or your core symbol) and test 5 variations over two weeks of replays. One variation will resonate with your eye and trading intuition—stick with it across live trading. Consistency in chart setup reduces cognitive load and prevents you from second-guessing your reading of order flow.
  • Enable "Tape alert tone" with volume-based intensity. Rather than a binary beep/no-beep, ATAS can vary alert tone pitch based on order size: large block trades trigger a high pitch, medium trades a middle pitch, small fills a low pitch. Your ears will subconsciously detect when institutional-sized orders execute (high pitch spike) while filtering retail noise. This auditory input accelerates order flow pattern recognition faster than visual scanning alone.
  • Combine backtest journaling with time-zone filtering. If you trade ES only during U.S. market hours, backtest only 9:30 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET data. If you trade crypto globally, separate your journal by timezone (Asia session results vs. London session vs. U.S. session). ATAS's journal filtering lets you instantly see which market session produces your highest win rate—then concentrate your live trading in that window.

Common ATAS Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue 1: "No data" or delayed quotes in footprint charts

This indicates your data connection dropped or never fully established. Solution: Check your broker connection (File > Settings > Data Connection). Confirm your API credentials are valid—many brokers' API keys expire after 90 days without use. If using free demo data, ensure you're connected to the internet with an active browser window open (some free data feeds require ATAS to maintain an open web session). Restart ATAS if the connection remains broken. For futures traders using Rithmic, contact your broker to confirm your account has live data enabled; some brokers require a separate request to enable real-time tick data.

Issue 2: Footprint charts show no clusters or very few trades

Usually means your volume filter is set too high, or you're viewing the wrong market. Navigate to the chart's Properties panel (right-click chart > Properties) and lower the "Minimum cluster size" from its current value to 1. Ensure you're viewing a symbol with actual trading volume—checking a micro-contract or a low-volume cryptocurrency at 3 a.m. will show sparse order flow. If using replay mode, confirm you're replaying during a high-volume period (market opens or major economic releases); slow market consolidations may appear quiet even in replay.

Issue 3: One-click trading in Smart DOM doesn't execute orders

Confirm your broker connection is live: your Smart DOM window should display actual market bid/ask prices, not zeros. If prices are missing, reconnect your data feed (File > Settings > Data Connection > Reconnect). Second, confirm your broker account has margin available or sufficient capital for your intended trade size—ATAS relays the order, but your broker rejects it if you lack margin. Third, ensure you've set position limits in ATAS: File > Settings > Trading > Max Position Size. If this limit is lower than your click size, ATAS blocks the order as a safety feature.

Issue 4: Windows 11 compatibility or lag

ATAS was built for Windows 7-10 and sometimes stutters on Windows 11 with multiple high-resolution monitors. Solution: Run ATAS in Windows 10 compatibility mode (right-click the ATAS.exe > Properties > Compatibility > Run this program in compatibility mode for: Windows 10). If you're seeing lag, reduce your chart's refresh rate: Properties > Update Interval: change from 100ms to 500ms. Close unnecessary background applications (Discord, Chrome with many tabs, Spotify)—every percentage of CPU matters for sub-millisecond order entry. If upgrading your hardware, prioritize GPU (dedicated graphics card) over CPU, as ATAS is graphics-bound for real-time chart rendering.

Is ATAS Worth It? Our Verdict

ATAS is worth the investment if you're committed to order flow and volume analysis as your core trading approach—the 400+ footprint cluster variations and 70+ specialized volume tools have no equivalent at Bookmap's (limited cluster configurations) or Sierra Chart's (traditional charting platform). At 4.3/5 rating with the free crypto tier fully usable for live trading, there's no financial barrier to testing whether footprint reading resonates with you. However, if you're a technical analysis trader content with moving averages and MACD, or if you trade exclusively on Mac or Linux, ATAS is overkill—invest in ThinkOrSwim's charting or NinjaTrader instead. Serious order flow traders upgrading from chart-based systems to institutional-grade Smart DOM and Smart Tape for $69.95/month (Pro) is a no-brainer. The real question isn't whether ATAS is worth it, but whether footprint trading is your edge—ATAS is simply the sharpest tool for that specific edge.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
